reasoning: Peer sheet is classic Denzi RPG pixel art: tight ~16px sprites with consistent black outlines, modest highlights, varied-but-grounded palette spanning greens/reds/browns/blues, high detail density relative to sprite size, and signature Denzi charm (distinct character silhouettes, expressive poses). Candidate vfx_explosion_2_v04 fails on 7 of 11 dims. (1) Outline weight/color: candidate has ZERO outline—edges dissolve into background pixels; every peer sprite carries a dark perimeter outline. (2) Rendering style: candidate uses oversized ~6-8px pixel blocks producing a chunky mobile-VFX look that clashes with peers' tight classical pixel art. (3) Palette overlap: candidate is almost entirely orange/gold/warm-yellow + light-blue glow—colors that appear nowhere in the peer sheet. (4) Color temperature: extreme warm bias vs. peers' neutral-to-cool mixed palette. (5) Highlight intensity: candidate is essentially a single overblown highlight mass; peers show restrained 1-2 highlight dots. (6) Detail density: given its large canvas the candidate is shockingly sparse—few distinct pixel clusters—opposite to peers' dense sprite work. (7) Charm level: it reads as a generic explosion burst with no character or wit; Denzi peers all have personality/silhouette identity. Passes only on shadow direction (both negligible) and shadow count (both ~0). Proportion difference noted but not penalized as per instructions. This asset would be a visual intruder in a Denzi-style scene.
